title: Nepeta yesoensis
taxonomy: Nepeta yesoensis is classified within the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This plant is a species within the Nepeta genus, which includes several other species of catmint. The genus Nepeta is further classified within the tribe Nepeteae, and the family Lamiaceae is part of the order Lamiales. The order Lamiales is a part of the clade asterids, which also includes other flowering plants such as mint, lavender, and sage. The classification of Nepeta yesoensis reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the asterid clade.
uses: Nepeta yesoensis, also known as Yeso catmint, has been reported to possess various medicinal properties. intro: Nepeta yesoensis, also known as Japanese catmint, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to Japan and Korea. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including anxiety and insomnia, due to its sedative and calming effects, which may be attributed to its ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. The bioactive constituents of Nepeta yesoensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Nepeta yesoensis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being dried and used as a calming herb in smoking blends and potpourri.
